description_official: DRAFT — replace with verified text from NAVMC 1200.1E or current equivalent. The Rifleman is the foundation of the Marine Corps infantry squad and the basic scheme of maneuver for the ground-combat element.
description_plain: The rifleman is the Marine the rest of the Corps exists to support. You close with the enemy on foot, lead fire teams, and become an expert with the service rifle. This is ground combat — small units, long days, and the discipline that comes from doing the same hard things until they're automatic. Training pipeline runs through Parris Island or San Diego, then School of Infantry East or West. Most 0311s end up in an infantry battalion at Camp Pendleton, Camp Lejeune, or Kaneohe Bay. Expect deployments.
description_tldr: The Marine Corps rifleman is the ground-combat backbone of the USMC infantry — small-unit leader, marksman, and the Marine everything else supports.

description_official: DRAFT — verify against current Marine Corps directive. Note: the Scout Sniper MOS has undergone restructuring in recent Marine Corps force design changes; verify current status before publishing.
description_plain: Scout snipers are the infantry's long-range eyes and precision-fire specialists. You earn this MOS by passing a selection course and Scout Sniper Basic Course — the washout rate is high and the training is hard on purpose. Status and force structure of this MOS have shifted with Force Design; confirm current standing before relying on anything here.
description_tldr: Marine Corps long-range reconnaissance and precision-fire specialist — status under review in recent Force Design changes.

physical_requirements.details: Enhanced physical screening for selection course; high-end PFT required.

training_pipeline.description: Must be a qualified rifleman before selection.

training_pipeline.location: Camp Lejeune, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA

description_official: DRAFT — pending verification against NAVMC 1200 series.
description_plain: You run the platoon's heavy and medium machine guns. Think M240, M2 .50 cal, and the support-by-fire position in almost every infantry maneuver. Expect to carry weight and to be the first thing the enemy wants to silence.
description_tldr: Marine infantry machine gunner — crew-served weapons specialist providing suppressive and sustained fire.

physical_requirements.details: Standard USMC requirements; strong emphasis on load-bearing capacity.

training_pipeline.location: Parris Island, SC or San Diego, CA
Camp Geiger, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA

description_plain: The 0369 is the senior enlisted infantry leader — platoon sergeant, company gunnery sergeant, battalion-level billets. You get here by staying in, leading well, and proving you can run complex infantry operations at the platoon and company level.
description_tldr: Senior enlisted Marine Corps infantry leader — platoon sergeant and above; the promotion progression for career infantrymen.

asvab.notes: Promotion-progression MOS; not an entry-level assignment.

training_pipeline.location: Camp Lejeune, NC or Camp Pendleton, CA
training_pipeline.prerequisite: Prior infantry MOS + E-5 or above

description_plain: Comms Marines. You set up and run the radio networks that infantry, logistics, and command elements depend on. Heavy emphasis on HF, VHF, UHF, and tactical satellite. Expect to be attached to infantry units as often as you're with a comms unit.
description_tldr: Marine Corps tactical radio operator — builds and runs voice and data networks for operational units.
